instructions.instruction: Run exploration phases in order (1-5), each building on the previous phase's findings
instructions.original_snippets: Run phases in order. Each phase builds on the previous one's findings.

instructions.instruction: Use subagents for phases 2-4 to keep the primary context clean
instructions.original_snippets: Use subagents for phases 2-4 to keep the primary context clean.
instructions.relevant_when: Executing exploration phases in an environment with subagent support

instructions.instruction: Phase 1 Inventory: paginate entries_list with limit 50 and increasing offset until all entries are covered
instructions.original_snippets: entries_list({ diary_id, limit: 50, offset: 0 }) ... entries_list({ diary_id, limit: 50, offset: 50 }) ... continue until all entries are covered
instructions.relevant_when: Running Phase 1 inventory of diary entries

instructions.instruction: Phase 1 computes: entry type counts, tag frequency, tag namespaces (grouped by prefix before first colon), importance distribution (histogram 1-10), temporal range
instructions.original_snippets: 1. Entry type counts ... 2. Tag frequency ... 3. Tag namespaces: group tags by prefix (everything before the first `:`) ... 4. Importance distribution: histogram ... 5. Temporal range

instructions.instruction: Tag namespaces must be discovered from the data, NOT hardcoded
instructions.original_snippets: Do NOT hardcode expected namespaces — discover them from the data.
instructions.relevant_when: Grouping tags by namespace in Phase 1

instructions.instruction: Phase 2 searches for agent mistakes using entries_list with incident tag first, then entries_search with episodic type as fallback
instructions.original_snippets: entries_list({ diary_id, tags: ["incident"], limit: 20 }) ... If no entries have the `incident` tag, fall back to: entries_search({ diary_id, query: "what happened root cause fix applied", entry_types: ["episodic"], limit: 20 })

instructions.instruction: For each episodic entry, extract: what went wrong, root cause, fix applied, preventive context, subsystem (from tags/content), severity (Critical/High/Medium/Low)
instructions.original_snippets: | What went wrong | The mistake or failure | ... | Root cause | Why it happened | ... | Severity | Critical / High / Medium / Low |

instructions.instruction: Group episodic findings by subsystem; highest-severity incidents with clear preventive context are best Task Harvest candidates
instructions.original_snippets: Group by subsystem. The highest-severity incidents with clear preventive context are the best Task Harvest candidates.

instructions.instruction: Phase 3 uses the most common procedural tag from Phase 1 to search for procedural entries, falling back to entries_search with query 'commit'
instructions.original_snippets: entries_list({ diary_id, limit: 30, tags: [<most common procedural tag from Phase 1>] }) ... If no obvious procedural tag exists, use: entries_search({ diary_id, query: "commit", entry_types: ["procedural"], limit: 30 })

instructions.instruction: Phase 3 checks for anti-patterns: double-prefix tags (e.g. scope:scope:*), catch-all tags, entries without branch or scope tags, unusually broad entries
instructions.original_snippets: Anti-patterns: look for double-prefix tags (e.g. `scope:scope:*`), catch-all tags, entries without branch or scope tags, unusually broad entries (many files changed)
instructions.relevant_when: Analyzing procedural entries for quality issues

instructions.instruction: Phase 4 coverage gaps: if learn:trace entries exist, analyze repeated questions with no context; otherwise compare codebase structure against diary topics
instructions.original_snippets: If learn:trace entries exist ... Analyze: which questions were asked repeatedly? ... If no learn:trace entries: compare the codebase structure against diary topics.

instructions.instruction: Phase 5 compile recipes must specify: name, intent, task_prompt, token_budget, lambda, w_importance, w_recency, include_tags, exclude_tags, rationale
instructions.original_snippets: name: '<descriptive name>' ... intent: '<what task this context supports>' ... task_prompt: '<specific question>' ... token_budget: <number> ... lambda: <0.0-1.0> ... w_importance: <0.0-1.0> ... w_recency: <0.0-1.0> ... include_tags ... exclude_tags ... rationale

instructions.instruction: Compile recipes must be based on what the diary actually contains — don't recommend filtering by tags that don't exist
instructions.original_snippets: Base recommendations on what the diary actually contains — don't recommend filtering by `source:tile` if no tiles exist, don't recommend excluding `learn:trace` if no learn traces exist.

instructions.instruction: Output findings as a diary entry with entry_type: reflection, tags: [exploration, diary-health], importance: 6
instructions.original_snippets: Write findings as a diary entry (`entry_type: reflection`) ... Tags: `["exploration", "diary-health"]` ... Importance: 6

instructions.instruction: Output follows a specific structure: Inventory table, Tag Namespaces tree, Agent Mistakes (grouped by severity), Commit Patterns, Coverage Gaps, Noise Sources, Recommended Compile Recipes
instructions.original_snippets: ## Inventory ... ## Tag Namespaces ... ## Agent Mistakes ... ## Commit Patterns ... ## Coverage Gaps ... ## Noise Sources ... ## Recommended Compile Recipes

instructions.instruction: Note relation opportunities after exploration: incidents proving anti-patterns, decisions referenced by commits, repeated incidents across branches
instructions.original_snippets: After exploration, note promising cross-type relation candidates: Incidents that prove scan entry anti-patterns ... Decisions referenced by procedural commits ... Repeated incidents (same bug pattern across branches)
instructions.relevant_when: Concluding the exploration and identifying follow-up work

instructions.instruction: Recovery after context compression: check for existing exploration entry via entries_search with exploration tag, read it, skip completed phases, resume from next incomplete phase
instructions.original_snippets: Check for existing exploration: entries_search({ diary_id, tags: ["exploration"], limit: 1 }) ... If found, read it and skip completed phases ... Resume from the next incomplete phase
instructions.relevant_when: Resuming an exploration after context window compression
